domains.why: A chandler-wracker feud fought in manifests is public sport: the harbormaster reads the paperwork, the Gull's landlord hears the captains bet on it, and the Arcade and the pens hear everything.
Onna feeds the mudlark and knows why his treasures never feed HIM; the pens and the Arcade price the hook like weather.
Salt Row trades stock with the Slop-Chest and can count whose shelves those really are; the Arcade and the pens counted first.
The quest's own rumor hooks (Withy sells the seeing, Herdis hears the pens) - and Goodman Saltgate watches the Netter house across the party wall with twenty years of lawyerly attention.
The Bilge pours for whoever the Gull spoils; the rival sergeant files the grudge under ammunition; the impound keeper is Watch in everything but the wage line.
A SECRET stays a secret: only the landlady who lost her lodger to that night, and the roofs - who bank what bankers bury - can speak it.
Night cargo crosses under the roofline's feet, the Gullet hears what the quay won't say, and the Wrackhouse logs what lands without a manifest.
A twenty-year lawyer feud is ward theatre; House Quayward follows it the way old money follows a slow shipwreck.
The Bilge's lamp sees the odd pair share a table below the waterline where names stop; Redda keeps it gray, mostly.
Watch-adjacent keepers read both sergeants' reports in forfeits and cells; the Bilge hears the toasts each sergeant's men drink AT the other.
The pens border Kennel Row and heard the original pup; the guardhouse inherits the feud in its paperwork; the Arcade remembers everything twenty years old.
Only the Mission's own - the night-soup sister and the faithful madman - stand close enough to both vigils to speak of the old promise, quietly.
The Lantern Room overlooks the Mission step, the Wrackhouse logs what the strand gives up (and to whom), and the pens find it sweet.
One workshop romantic recognizes another; the Bilge counts the cooper's walks past its door; the Arcade counts the barrels.
Salt Row returns the favor, the Gull's landlord minds a rival taproom's hours, and the pens simply like this one.

notes: Sprint-4 WORLD rumor knowledge-domains ('the rumor verb'): WHO can gossip WHICH micro-history beyond its own two parties. A history's parties always know their own story (implicit - never re-declared here; the bake fails loudly on a knower that is already a party). Knowers are notables.json ids, validated at bake against the bound notable map; each history appears at most once. These domains feed AskTopicsBake -> DocksPopulation.askTopicsOf(actorId) -> BarkSelector.selectAsk (the S4 SIM topic seam): the client passes the baked (notableId, historyIds) lists and the selector rotates the soul's speakable stories on the pinned ASK_TOPIC_DRAW_INDEX lane - sim-draw-free, presentation only. Domain logic is authored from leanings.json/notables.json canon: Herdis ('gossip moves through her pen') and Grandmother Withy ('decides what seeing is worth') are the ward's two wide ears; SECRETS stay tight (gilt-crell-erasure: only sedge + finch); romances travel through the counterpart trades. DocksAskTopicsTest is the reachability gate: with these domains no authored personal.*/gossip.* table is dead content.
